selected_article.original_title: 리퀴드 AI, '갤럭시 S25 울트라'에서 작동하는 450M VLM 출시
selected_article.reason: 스마트폰에서 직접 돌아가는 AI 모델은 매우 실용적이고 구체적인 소식이다. 클라우드 없이 기기에서 바로 이미지를 이해하는 기술은 개발자와 일반 사용자 모두에게 흥미로우며, 갤럭시 S25 울트라라는 구체적인 기기명으로 체감도가 높다.

content.post_main: 리퀴드 AI가 갤럭시 S25 울트라에서 바로 돌아가는 VLM 모델을 출시했다. 4.5억 파라미터 크기인데 클라우드 연결 없이 스마트폰에서 이미지를 보고 이해한다. 인터넷 끊어져도 사진 설명하고 질문 답변 가능하다는 뜻이다. 이제 AI가 진짜 내 폰 안에서 작동하는 시대가 왔다. 데이터 유출 걱정도 없고 응답 속도도 빠르다.
replies: 기존엔 ChatGPT나 클로드 쓰려면 서버로 데이터 전송해야 했는데, 이젠 폰 안에서 모든 처리가 끝난다.
리퀴드 AI는 액체 신경망으로 유명한데, 기존 트랜스포머보다 메모리 효율이 좋다고 알려져 있다.
갤럭시 S25 울트라 스펙이면 다른 플래그십 폰들도 충분히 돌릴 수 있을 것 같다. 온디바이스 AI 시대가 본격 시작되는 느낌이다.
개발자라면 허깅페이스에서 LFM2.5-VL-450M 모델 찾아보고, 실제 성능이 어느 정도인지 테스트해볼 만하다.

media_plan.reason: 스마트폰에서 실시간으로 이미지를 인식하고 답변하는 데모 영상이 있다면 온디바이스 AI의 실용성을 직관적으로 보여줄 수 있다

Posts from this author will be added to your daily email digest and your homepage feed. Posts from this author will be added to your daily email digest and your homepage feed. Coachella kicked off on Friday, and as usual, it’s the place to be for online influencers looking to show off their memorable experiences at the festival. A quick scroll through my social media feeds has already shown me many uncannily attractive figures in glitzy outfits, posing for perfectly staged photographs with celebrities. Only some of these content creators aren’t really there. They don’t even exist at all outside of our screens. They’re generated using AI tools. Faking Coachella attendance is nothing new — even real influencers have been doing so in recent years — but generative AI has now progressed to the point where it’s become much harder to pick these synthetic creators out amid the flood of real festival attendees. candidate_articles.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
candidate_articles.summary: Stanford’s latest AI Index shows a widening gap between experts and the public, with rising anxiety over jobs, healthcare, and the economy.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . AI experts and the public’s opinion on the technology are increasingly diverging, according to Stanford University’s annual report on the AI industry, which was released Monday. In particular, the report noted a growing trend of anxiety around AI and, in the U.S., concerns about how the technology will impact key societal areas, such as jobs, medical care, and the economy. The report’s findings follow growing negative sentiment about AI, with Gen Z reportedly leading the way, according to a recent Gallup poll . The study found that young people were growing less hopeful and more angry about the technology, even though around half of the demographic was using AI either daily or weekly. For some working in tech, the AI backlash has come as a surprise. AI leaders have focused on managing the possibility of Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI — a theoretical form of AI superintelligence that could perform any task a human could do and think for itself. But everyday folks are more concerned about AI’s impact on their paycheck and whether or not their power bills will go up as energy-hungry data centers are built . tbh it's weird that parts of the tech industry are still shocked by ongoing anti-AI public narratives when the leaders of OpenAI and Anthropic are like "if we do nothing this is going to suck for a lot of people", what do you think the sentiment is going to be? selected_article.original_title: 리퀴드 AI, '갤럭시 S25 울트라'에서 작동하는 450M VLM 출시
selected_article.reason: This article still offers the most concrete practical value - showing AI becoming truly portable. The technical details are specific enough to be useful for developers while accessible to general users.

content.post_main: 리퀴드 AI가 스마트폰에서 돌아가는 초경량 AI 모델 'LFM2.5-VL-450M'을 공개했다. 4억5천만 매개변수 규모로 갤럭시 S25 울트라 같은 기기에서 클라우드 연결 없이 이미지와 영상을 실시간으로 이해한다. VLM은 사진 보고 텍스트로 설명해주는 비전-언어 모델이다. 진짜 오프라인 AI 시대 시작인 듯.
replies: 지금까지는 이런 기능 쓰려면 사진을 서버로 보내서 처리했는데, 이제는 기기 자체에서 바로 처리한다.
요리할 때 냉장고 재료 사진 찍으면 레시피 추천하고, 병원에서 의료진이 X레이 보면서 바로 소견 확인하고.
개인정보 유출 걱정 없고, 인터넷 끊겨도 작동한다. 다만 450M 파라미터라 복잡한 추론은 아직 한계가 있을 듯.
임베디드 장치에서도 돌아간다니까 IoT, 로봇, 자율주행까지 활용 범위가 엄청 넓다. 개발자들은 이제 엣지 AI 앱을 본격적으로 만들 수 있겠네.

Posts from this author will be added to your daily email digest and your homepage feed. Posts from this author will be added to your daily email digest and your homepage feed. Coachella kicked off on Friday, and as usual, it’s the place to be for online influencers looking to show off their memorable experiences at the festival. A quick scroll through my social media feeds has already shown me many uncannily attractive figures in glitzy outfits, posing for perfectly staged photographs with celebrities. Only some of these content creators aren’t really there. They don’t even exist at all outside of our screens. They’re generated using AI tools. Faking Coachella attendance is nothing new — even real influencers have been doing so in recent years — but generative AI has now progressed to the point where it’s become much harder to pick these synthetic creators out amid the flood of real festival attendees. Some make little effort to hide that the “faces” of such social media accounts are entirely synthetic, tagging their bios and posts with disclosures that proudly identify the page’s personas as so-called “digital creators,” a term that obscures whether the pers

candidate_articles.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
candidate_articles.summary: Stanford’s latest AI Index shows a widening gap between experts and the public, with rising anxiety over jobs, healthcare, and the economy.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . AI experts and the public’s opinion on the technology are increasingly diverging, according to Stanford University’s annual report on the AI industry, which was released Monday. In particular, the report noted a growing trend of anxiety around AI and, in the U.S., concerns about how the technology will impact key societal areas, such as jobs, medical care, and the economy. The report’s findings follow growing negative sentiment about AI, with Gen Z reportedly leading the way, according to a recent Gallup poll . The study found that young people were growing less hopeful and more angry about the technology, even though around half of the demographic was using AI either daily or weekly. For some working in tech, the AI backlash has come as a surprise. AI leaders have focused on managing the possibility of Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI — a theoretical form of AI superintelligence that could perform any task a human could do and think for itself. But everyday folks are more concerned about AI’s impact on their paycheck and whether or not their power bills will go up as energy-hungry data centers are built . tbh it's weird that parts of the tech industry are still shocked by ongoing anti-AI public narratives when the leaders of OpenAI and Anthropic are like "if we do nothing this is going to suck for a lot of people", what do you think the sentiment is going to be? candidate_articles.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
candidate_articles.summary: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . OpenAI has acquired personal finance startup Hiro Finance, founder Ethan Bloch announced on Monday and OpenAI confirmed to TechCrunch. The startup was backed by A-list fintech VC firm Ribbit, as well as General Catalyst and Restive. Terms of the acquisition were not disclosed, nor did Hiro ever disclose how much money it raised. Since Hiro said it will be shutting down its operations on April 20 and deleting all data from its servers on May 13, we’re going to call this an acquihire. Bloch said in his post that Hiro employees are coming with him to OpenAI. He didn’t specify how many employees that entails, but LinkedIn lists about 10 people associated with the company. Bloch did not respond to our request for comment. The company was founded in 2023 and launched its AI tool about five months ago. Hiro offered AI-powered financial planning for consumers. Users entered financial information like salary, debts, and monthly costs, and the app modeled different what-if scenarios to help them make financial decisions. Hiro was specifically trained to nail financial math, including an option that allowed users to verify accuracy, Bloch said in a demo of the product. Over the past couple of years, state-of-the-art frontier models have gotten significantly better (even good) at math of all kinds. But historically, they haven’t been. candidate_articles.summary: 최근 국내외 엔지니어 사이에서는 '하네스(Harness)'라는 말이 많이 사용되고 있습니다. 하네스란 마구, 즉 말을 제어하기 위해 채우는 고삐와 안장 등을 의미합니다. 여기에서 파생된 하네스 엔지니어링은 AI가 적절하고 효율적으로 작업을 수행해 원하는 결과를 달성할 수 있도록 제어하고 관리하는 것을 의미합니다.이 말이 중요해진 것은 이제 AI 성능이 단순한 답변의 정확도를 넘어, 복잡하고 다단계인 업무 플로우를 수행하는 '에이전트'의 역할이 강조되기 때문입니다. 현재 AI는 복잡한 업무를 처리하는 과정에서 실패할 확률이 높습니다.

Much more shareable than the Claude controversy, with clear practical implications users can act on immediately. content.post_main: OpenAI가 개인 금융 계획 스타트업 '히로(Hiro)'를 인수했다. 히로는 AI로 개인 재정 시뮬레이션을 해주는 서비스였는데, 이제 ChatGPT에 금융 플래닝 기능이 들어올 것 같다. 사실상 아쿠이하이어로, 직원들이 OpenAI로 이동한다고. 금융 수학에 특화된 AI 모델을 확보한 셈이다.
replies: 히로는 작년에 출시됐는데, 급여·부채·생활비를 입력하면 다양한 시나리오를 시뮬레이션해줬다. 금융 계산 정확도 검증 기능도 있었고.
창업자가 디지트(Digit) 창업자 이선 블로흐다. 디지트도 자동 저축 앱으로 유명했으니, 금융 AI 분야에서는 검증된 인물인셈.
ChatGPT로 가계부 정리나 투자 계획 세우는 기능이 곧 나올 수도. 지금도 금융 조언은 하지만, 전문 도구가 통합되면 훨씬 정교해질 것 같다.

candidate_articles.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
candidate_articles.summary: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . OpenAI has acquired personal finance startup Hiro Finance, founder Ethan Bloch announced on Monday and OpenAI confirmed to TechCrunch. The startup was backed by A-list fintech VC firm Ribbit, as well as General Catalyst and Restive. Terms of the acquisition were not disclosed, nor did Hiro ever disclose how much money it raised. Since Hiro said it will be shutting down its operations on April 20 and deleting all data from its servers on May 13, we’re going to call this an acquihire. Bloch said in his post that Hiro employees are coming with him to OpenAI. He didn’t specify how many employees that entails, but LinkedIn lists about 10 people associated with the company. Bloch did not respond to our request for comment. The company was founded in 2023 and launched its AI tool about five months ago. Hiro offered AI-powered financial planning for consumers. Users entered financial information like salary, debts, and monthly costs, and the app modeled different what-if scenarios to help them make financial decisions. Hiro was specifically trained to nail financial math, including an option that allowed users to verify accuracy, Bloch said in a demo of the product. Over the past couple of years, state-of-the-art frontier models have gotten significantly better (even good) at math of all kinds. But historically, they haven’t been. This deal stands out for a couple of reasons. Bloch previously founded Digit, a di

candidate_articles.title: [4월13일] AI 엔지니어링의 새로운 기준 '하네스'..."모델 성능 넘어 시스템이 경쟁력"
candidate_articles.summary: 최근 국내외 엔지니어 사이에서는 '하네스(Harness)'라는 말이 많이 사용되고 있습니다. 하네스란 마구, 즉 말을 제어하기 위해 채우는 고삐와 안장 등을 의미합니다. 여기에서 파생된 하네스 엔지니어링은 AI가 적절하고 효율적으로 작업을 수행해 원하는 결과를 달성할 수 있도록 제어하고 관리하는 것을 의미합니다.이 말이 중요해진 것은 이제 AI 성능이 단순한 답변의 정확도를 넘어, 복잡하고 다단계인 업무 플로우를 수행하는 '에이전트'의 역할이 강조되기 때문입니다. 현재 AI는 복잡한 업무를 처리하는 과정에서 실패할 확률이 높습니다.

candidate_articles.title: 메타, 구글 제치고 디지털 광고 수익 세계 1위 달성 전망
candidate_articles.summary: 메타가 글로벌 디지털 광고 시장에서 처음으로 구글을 제치고 1위에 오를 것으로 전망됐다. selected_article.original_title: 컴퓨팅 수요 폭발로 GPU 비용 대폭 상승...두달 만에 50% 올라
selected_article.reason: This article addresses a critical practical issue that directly affects developers and businesses using AI. The 50% increase in two months is a concrete, shocking statistic that makes for highly shareable content with immediate practical implications.

content.post_main: GPU 비용이 두 달 만에 50% 폭등했다. 에이전트형 AI 때문이다. 단순 챗봇과 달리 코딩, 일정관리, 업무처리를 자율적으로 하는 AI들이 훨씬 많은 연산을 요구하거든. AI 스타트업들은 서비스 장애, 제품 출시 지연까지 겪고 있다고. 이제 AI 프로젝트 예산 짤 때 컴퓨팅 비용부터 확인해야 할 듯.
replies: 챗봇은 질문-답변 한 번이면 끝이지만, 에이전트는 계속 생각하고 판단하고 실행해야 한다. 연산량이 몇 배는 차이난다.
특히 코딩 에이전트들이 문제다. 코드 짜고 테스트하고 디버깅하는 과정에서 GPU를 계속 물고 늘어진다.
개인 개발자라면 로컬 모델이나 더 저렴한 API 찾아보고, 기업이라면 컴퓨팅 비용 예산을 2배로 잡는 게 안전할 듯.
언제까지 오를지 모르겠다. 에이전트 AI가 본격 확산되는 중이라 당분간은 공급 부족 상태 지속될 것 같다.

candidate_articles.title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
candidate_articles.summary: One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI's recent round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic's current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . OpenAI’s $852 billion valuation is facing skepticism from some of its own investors as the company scrambles to reorient itself around enterprise customers and fend off Anthropic, according to the Financial Times . Anthropic’s annualized revenue jumped from $9 billion at the end of 2025 to $30 billion by the end of March, driven largely by demand for its coding tools. One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI’s round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic’s current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain. The secondary market tells a similar story right now, where demand for Anthropic shares has grown nearly insatiable while OpenAI shares are trading at a discount . Altman has been here before. During his tenure leading Y Combinator, aggressive valuation inflation left some portfolio companies financially stranded while others proved worth every penny and then some. OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar pushed back, telling the FT that the company’s $122 billion raise — the largest private fundraising in history — was evidence of continued investor confidence. Not everyone is persuaded. Jai Das, president of investment firm Sapphire Ventures (who has no stake in either company) told the FT he saw OpenAI as “the Netscape of AI,” a reference to the once-dominant browser that was overtaken by Microsoft and eventually absorbed by AOL.

content.post_main: 앤트로픽이 이번 주 '클로드 오퍼스 4.7'과 함께 웹사이트·프레젠테이션 자동 제작 AI 도구를 출시한다. 기존 클로드와 달리 디자인 작업을 직접 처리할 수 있게 된다는 뜻이다. 플래그십 모델이지만 최상위는 아니라고 하니, 더 강력한 '미소스'를 염두에 둔 단계별 출시 전략으로 보인다.
replies: 가장 주목할 부분은 웹사이트와 PPT 자동 생성 기능이다. 기획서 하나 던져주면 완성된 프레젠테이션이 나온다면 업무 방식이 완전히 바뀔 것 같다.
그런데 '플래그십이지만 최상위 아님'이라는 표현이 흥미롭다. 더 강력한 미소스는 보안 우려로 제한 테스트 중이라고 하니, 성능 조절해서 내놓는 셈이네.
디자인 도구 개발하시는 분들은 이번 출시를 주목해볼 만하다. 클로드가 직접 디자인까지 처리하면 기존 워크플로우 도구들과의 경쟁 구도가 바뀔 수도 있거든.

candidate_articles.title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
candidate_articles.summary: One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI's recent round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic's current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . OpenAI’s $852 billion valuation is facing skepticism from some of its own investors as the company scrambles to reorient itself around enterprise customers and fend off Anthropic, according to the Financial Times . Anthropic’s annualized revenue jumped from $9 billion at the end of 2025 to $30 billion by the end of March, driven largely by demand for its coding tools. One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI’s round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic’s current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain. The secondary market tells a similar story right now, where demand for Anthropic shares has grown nearly insatiable while OpenAI shares are trading at a discount . Altman has been here before. During his tenure leading Y Combinator, aggressive valuation inflation left some portfolio companies financially stranded while others proved worth every penny and then some. OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar pushed back, telling the FT that the company’s $122 billion raise — the largest private fundraising in history — was evidence of continued investor confidence. Not everyone is persuaded. Jai Das, president of investment firm Sapphire Ventures (who has no stake in either company) told the FT he saw OpenAI as “the Netscape of AI,” a reference to the once-dominant browser that was overtaken by Microsoft and eventually absorbed by AOL.

candidate_articles.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
candidate_articles.summary: Allbirds is ditching wool sneakers for AI servers, rebranding as NewBird AI after locking in a $50M convertible financing facility.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . After selling its shoe brand and assets last month for $39 million , Allbirds is pivoting to AI. Of course, the company is also changing its name, since the footwear brand “Allbirds” was part of the sale. Introducing: NewBird AI, a “fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service and AI-native cloud solutions provider,” the company announced via its investor relations site on Wednesday. The rebranded AI company also announced a $50 million investment from an undisclosed institutional investor in the form of a convertible financing facility. It’s objectively pretty funny that Allbirds is becoming an AI company — not because it’s unusual for companies to pivot, but because of how extreme this pivot is. The maker of the shoes once craved by the Silicon Valley tech set is now going to be a provider of GPUs. It’s somewhat absurd — and risky — but you can see how the business came to this decision. After the asset and brand sale, Allbirds can keep the public company’s shell (it’s been traded on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol “BIRD”) and then reuse it to invest in the hot AI sector. This recalls the time in 2017 when the Long Island Iced Tea company pivoted to the blockchain , prompting the stock to jump some 275% after the rebranding. That pivot didn’t pan out, as the NASDAQ stock exchange delisted the stock the following year after Bitcoin fever died down. Allbirds-turned-NewBird is likely hoping for a different outcome. T

selected_article.reason: This remains the best choice for practical usefulness. The feedback points help create a more actionable post that developers and regular users can immediately apply to their workflow.

content.post_main:
구글 크롬에 'AI 스킬' 기능이 추가됐다. 자주 쓰는 AI 프롬프트를 저장해서 여러 웹페이지에서 재사용할 수 있다.

기존 제미나이는 웹페이지를 요약하거나 질문하는 정도였다면, 이제는 내가 만든 프롬프트 템플릿을 저장해서 계속 활용할 수 있다. 매번 똑같은 명령어를 다시 칠 필요가 없어진다.
replies: 사용법은 간단하다. 크롬에서 제미나이를 열고 프롬프트를 입력한 다음, '스킬로 저장' 버튼을 누르면 끝.
개발자라면 "이 코드의 잠재적 보안 취약점을 찾아서 개선 방법과 함께 설명해줘" 같은 복잡한 프롬프트를 저장해두면 유용하다.
"회의록을 액션 아이템별로 정리해줘", "기술 문서를 비개발자도 이해할 수 있게 다시 써줘" 같은 업무용 템플릿을 만들어보자.

candidate_articles.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
candidate_articles.summary: Allbirds is ditching wool sneakers for AI servers, rebranding as NewBird AI after locking in a $50M convertible financing facility.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . After selling its shoe brand and assets last month for $39 million , Allbirds is pivoting to AI. Of course, the company is also changing its name, since the footwear brand “Allbirds” was part of the sale. Introducing: NewBird AI, a “fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service and AI-native cloud solutions provider,” the company announced via its investor relations site on Wednesday. The rebranded AI company also announced a $50 million investment from an undisclosed institutional investor in the form of a convertible financing facility. It’s objectively pretty funny that Allbirds is becoming an AI company — not because it’s unusual for companies to pivot, but because of how extreme this pivot is. The maker of the shoes once craved by the Silicon Valley tech set is now going to be a provider of GPUs. It’s somewhat absurd — and risky — but you can see how the business came to this decision. After the asset and brand sale, Allbirds can keep the public company’s shell (it’s been traded on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol “BIRD”) and then reuse it to invest in the hot AI sector. This recalls the time in 2017 when the Long Island Iced Tea company pivoted to the blockchain , prompting the stock to jump some 275% after the rebranding. That pivot didn’t pan out, as the NASDAQ stock exchange delisted the stock the following year after Bitcoin fever died down. Allbirds-turned-NewBird is likely hoping for a different outcome. T

candidate_articles.title: OpenAI updates its Agents SDK to help enterprises build safer, more capable agents
candidate_articles.summary: OpenAI has expanded the capabilities of its agent-building toolkit, as agentic AI continues to grow in popularity.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . Agentic AI is the tech industry’s newest success story, and companies like OpenAI and Anthropic are racing to give enterprises the tools they need to create these automated little helpers. To that end, OpenAI has now updated its agents software development toolkit (SDK) , introducing a number of new features designed to help businesses create their own agents that run on the backs of OpenAI’s models. The SDK’s new capabilities include a sandboxing ability, which allows the agents to operate in controlled computer environments. This is important because running agents in a totally unsupervised fashion can be risky due to their occasionally unpredictable nature. With the sandbox integration, agents can work in a siloed capacity within a particular workspace, accessing files and code only for particular operations, while otherwise protecting the system’s overall integrity. Relatedly, the new version of the SDK also provides developers with an in-distribution harness for frontier models that will allow those agents to work with files and approved tools within a workspace, the company said. (In agent development, the “harness” is a term that refers to the other components of an agent besides the model that it’s running on. An in-distribution harness often allows companies to both deploy and test the agents running on frontier models, which are considered to be the most advanced, general-purpose models availab

Google is launching a new Gemini app on Mac that allows you to interact with the AI assistant without switching windows on your desktop. With the app, you can use the Option + Space shortcut to pull up a floating chat bubble, where you can ask Gemini questions and share your window. Before sharing your window, you’ll need to give Gemini permission to access your system’s information before sharing your window. From there, the AI assistant will pull information from what you’re looking at to help answer your question. It looks a bit like Apple’s upgraded version of Spotlight , which you can now use to perform actions on your device, as well as access AI models like OpenAI’s ChatGPT. The new app puts Google in the running to compete with Anthropic , OpenAI , and Perplexity, all of which want their chatbots to become the go-to AI model on desktop devices. candidate_articles.title: Hightouch reaches $100M ARR fueled by marketing tools powered by AI
candidate_articles.summary: The startup says it grew its ARR by $70 million in just 20 months after launching an AI agent platform for marketers.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . Historically, marketers relied on designers and other creative professionals to develop images and videos for personalized online ad campaigns. In late 2024, seven-year-old startup Hightouch launched an AI-powered service that allows marketing professionals to create custom content for brands such as Domino’s, Chime, PetSmart, and Spotify without involving brand design teams or ad agencies. The offering has been highly successful. Since introducing its AI product 20 months ago, Hightouch has added $70 million in annualized recurring revenue (ARR), it tells TechCrunch, bringing the startup to a total of $100 million in ARR. “Before GenAI, it was impossible for someone without many, many years of design skills to create consumer-level assets,” said Kashish Gupta, Hightouch’s co-CEO. The company is also led by co-CEO Tejas Manohar, a former engineering manager at Segment, a customer data platform acquired by Twilio for $3.2 billion in 2020. However, Hightouch’s approach goes beyond what standard AI models can do on their own. Hightouch says that many brands initially attempted to generate ad campaigns using general foundational models — broad AI systems that power tools like chatbots but lack knowledge of specific brands — only to find the resulting images and videos failed to meet “on-brand” standards. “Foundation models didn’t know about specific consumer brands, whether it was colors or fonts, tone, or a

candidate_articles.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet
candidate_articles.summary: LinkedIn says hiring is down 20% since 2022, but blames higher interest rates — not AI — for the slowdown.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . LinkedIn’s Blake Lawit, the chief global affairs and legal officer of the Microsoft-owned professional networking site, confirmed in an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week that the company’s data shows a decline in hiring of around 20% since 2022. However, he pushed back at the idea that AI was to blame. “At LinkedIn… we have an economic graph which is over a billion members. We’ve got companies, jobs, skills. It’s really an amazing real-time view of what’s happening in the labor market. And we’ve looked — because everyone wants to know the answer to this question: Is AI impacting jobs right now? We’ve looked and, honestly, we haven’t seen it,” he said during his interview. Instead, the executive suggested that the decline in hiring was more closely tied to a rise in interest rates. “We have not seen the sort of impacts that you would expect to see in areas that everyone is talking about AI… like industries, whether or not it’s customer support, or administrative, or marketing — all these places that if we were seeing impacts [from] AI that’s where it would be,” Lawit continued. “Yes, hiring’s down, but not down more,” he added. Lawit also noted that LinkedIn’s data didn’t indicate that the decline in hiring of college-aged young adults getting their first jobs was “down more,” either, when compared with people who were in the middle of or later in their careers. content.post_main: 구글이 맥용 제미나이 네이티브 앱을 출시했다. Option + Space로 어디서나 떠오르는 채팅창을 띄울 수 있고, 현재 보고 있는 화면을 공유해서 질문할 수 있다. 브라우저 탭 전환 없이 바로 AI에게 물어볼 수 있게 된 거다. 코딩 중 에러 화면 공유하거나 엑셀 데이터 분석 요청할 때 정말 편할 듯.
replies: 화면 공유 전에는 시스템 정보 접근 권한을 허용해야 한다. 개인정보 보호 측면에서 주의할 점이긴 하다.
ChatGPT 맥 앱, 클로드 맥 앱에 이어 구글도 데스크톱 AI 경쟁에 본격 뛰어들었다. 이제 브라우저 없이도 AI 쓰는 게 당연해지겠다.
맥 앱스토어에서 바로 다운로드 가능하다. 설치하고 Option + Space 단축키부터 익혀두면 업무 효율이 확실히 올라갈 것 같다.

candidate_articles.title: OpenAI updates its Agents SDK to help enterprises build safer, more capable agents
candidate_articles.summary: OpenAI has expanded the capabilities of its agent-building toolkit, as agentic AI continues to grow in popularity.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . Agentic AI is the tech industry’s newest success story, and companies like OpenAI and Anthropic are racing to give enterprises the tools they need to create these automated little helpers. To that end, OpenAI has now updated its agents software development toolkit (SDK) , introducing a number of new features designed to help businesses create their own agents that run on the backs of OpenAI’s models. The SDK’s new capabilities include a sandboxing ability, which allows the agents to operate in controlled computer environments. This is important because running agents in a totally unsupervised fashion can be risky due to their occasionally unpredictable nature. With the sandbox integration, agents can work in a siloed capacity within a particular workspace, accessing files and code only for particular operations, while otherwise protecting the system’s overall integrity. Relatedly, the new version of the SDK also provides developers with an in-distribution harness for frontier models that will allow those agents to work with files and approved tools within a workspace, the company said. (In agent development, the “harness” is a term that refers to the other components of an agent besides the model that it’s running on. An in-distribution harness often allows companies to both deploy and test the agents running on frontier models, which are considered to be the most advanced, general-purpose models availab

Google is launching a new Gemini app on Mac that allows you to interact with the AI assistant without switching windows on your desktop. With the app, you can use the Option + Space shortcut to pull up a floating chat bubble, where you can ask Gemini questions and share your window. Before sharing your window, you’ll need to give Gemini permission to access your system’s information before sharing your window. From there, the AI assistant will pull information from what you’re looking at to help answer your question. It looks a bit like Apple’s upgraded version of Spotlight , which you can now use to perform actions on your device, as well as access AI models like OpenAI’s ChatGPT. The new app puts Google in the running to compete with Anthropic , OpenAI , and Perplexity, all of which want their chatbots to become the go-to AI model on desktop devices. candidate_articles.title: Hightouch reaches $100M ARR fueled by marketing tools powered by AI
candidate_articles.summary: The startup says it grew its ARR by $70 million in just 20 months after launching an AI agent platform for marketers.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . Historically, marketers relied on designers and other creative professionals to develop images and videos for personalized online ad campaigns. In late 2024, seven-year-old startup Hightouch launched an AI-powered service that allows marketing professionals to create custom content for brands such as Domino’s, Chime, PetSmart, and Spotify without involving brand design teams or ad agencies. The offering has been highly successful. Since introducing its AI product 20 months ago, Hightouch has added $70 million in annualized recurring revenue (ARR), it tells TechCrunch, bringing the startup to a total of $100 million in ARR. “Before GenAI, it was impossible for someone without many, many years of design skills to create consumer-level assets,” said Kashish Gupta, Hightouch’s co-CEO. The company is also led by co-CEO Tejas Manohar, a former engineering manager at Segment, a customer data platform acquired by Twilio for $3.2 billion in 2020. However, Hightouch’s approach goes beyond what standard AI models can do on their own. Hightouch says that many brands initially attempted to generate ad campaigns using general foundational models — broad AI systems that power tools like chatbots but lack knowledge of specific brands — only to find the resulting images and videos failed to meet “on-brand” standards. “Foundation models didn’t know about specific consumer brands, whether it was colors or fonts, tone, or a

candidate_articles.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet
candidate_articles.summary: LinkedIn says hiring is down 20% since 2022, but blames higher interest rates — not AI — for the slowdown.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . LinkedIn’s Blake Lawit, the chief global affairs and legal officer of the Microsoft-owned professional networking site, confirmed in an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week that the company’s data shows a decline in hiring of around 20% since 2022. However, he pushed back at the idea that AI was to blame. “At LinkedIn… we have an economic graph which is over a billion members. We’ve got companies, jobs, skills. It’s really an amazing real-time view of what’s happening in the labor market. And we’ve looked — because everyone wants to know the answer to this question: Is AI impacting jobs right now? We’ve looked and, honestly, we haven’t seen it,” he said during his interview. Instead, the executive suggested that the decline in hiring was more closely tied to a rise in interest rates. “We have not seen the sort of impacts that you would expect to see in areas that everyone is talking about AI… like industries, whether or not it’s customer support, or administrative, or marketing — all these places that if we were seeing impacts [from] AI that’s where it would be,” Lawit continued. “Yes, hiring’s down, but not down more,” he added. Lawit also noted that LinkedIn’s data didn’t indicate that the decline in hiring of college-aged young adults getting their first jobs was “down more,” either, when compared with people who were in the middle of or later in their careers. With the sandbox integration, agents can work in a siloed capacity within a particular workspace, accessing files and code only for particular operations, while otherwise protecting the system’s overall integrity. Relatedly, the new version of the SDK also provides developers with an in-distribution harness for frontier models that will allow those agents to work with files and approved tools within a workspace, the company said. (In agent development, the “harness” is a term that refers to the other components of an agent besides the model that it’s running on. An in-distribution harness often allows companies to both deploy and test the agents running on frontier models, which are considered to be the most advanced, general-purpose models availab

REGISTER NOW . Historically, marketers relied on designers and other creative professionals to develop images and videos for personalized online ad campaigns. In late 2024, seven-year-old startup Hightouch launched an AI-powered service that allows marketing professionals to create custom content for brands such as Domino’s, Chime, PetSmart, and Spotify without involving brand design teams or ad agencies. The offering has been highly successful. Since introducing its AI product 20 months ago, Hightouch has added $70 million in annualized recurring revenue (ARR), it tells TechCrunch, bringing the startup to a total of $100 million in ARR. “Before GenAI, it was impossible for someone without many, many years of design skills to create consumer-level assets,” said Kashish Gupta, Hightouch’s co-CEO. The company is also led by co-CEO Tejas Manohar, a former engineering manager at Segment, a customer data platform acquired by Twilio for $3.2 billion in 2020. However, Hightouch’s approach goes beyond what standard AI models can do on their own. Hightouch says that many brands initially attempted to generate ad campaigns using general foundational models — broad AI systems that power tools like chatbots but lack knowledge of specific brands — only to find the resulting images and videos failed to meet “on-brand” standards. “Foundation models didn’t know about specific consumer brands, whether it was colors or fonts, tone, or a

candidate_articles.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet

LinkedIn’s Blake Lawit, the chief global affairs and legal officer of the Microsoft-owned professional networking site, confirmed in an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week that the company’s data shows a decline in hiring of around 20% since 2022. However, he pushed back at the idea that AI was to blame. “At LinkedIn… we have an economic graph which is over a billion members. We’ve got companies, jobs, skills. It’s really an amazing real-time view of what’s happening in the labor market. And we’ve looked — because everyone wants to know the answer to this question: Is AI impacting jobs right now? We’ve looked and, honestly, we haven’t seen it,” he said during his interview. Instead, the executive suggested that the decline in hiring was more closely tied to a rise in interest rates. “We have not seen the sort of impacts that you would expect to see in areas that everyone is talking about AI… like industries, whether or not it’s customer support, or administrative, or marketing — all these places that if we were seeing impacts [from] AI that’s where it would be,” Lawit continued. “Yes, hiring’s down, but not down more,” he added. Lawit also noted that LinkedIn’s data didn’t indicate that the decline in hiring of college-aged young adults getting their first jobs was “down more,” either, when compared with people who were in the middle of or later in their careers. content.post_main: OpenAI가 기업용 에이전트 SDK에 '샌드박스' 기능을 추가했다. 이제 AI 에이전트가 격리된 컴퓨터 환경에서만 작동하고, 특정 파일과 코드에만 접근한다. 전체 시스템은 보호받으면서도 에이전트는 필요한 작업을 수행할 수 있다. 그동안 에이전트의 예측 불가능한 동작 때문에 기업에서 쓰기 어려웠는데, 이제 안전하게 배포 가능해졌다.
replies: 샌드박스에서 에이전트는 지정된 워크스페이스 내 파일 읽기/쓰기, 승인된 도구 사용만 가능하다. 시스템 파일이나 다른 프로젝트는 접근 불가.
기존에는 에이전트 테스트할 때마다 전체 시스템 위험을 감수해야 했다. 이제 sandbox 환경에서 먼저 검증하고 프로덕션에 올릴 수 있다.
SDK는 '인-디스트리뷰션 하네스'도 제공한다. 최신 AI 모델에서 에이전트를 배포하고 테스트하는 프레임워크다. 개발 속도가 훨씬 빨라질 예정.
기업 개발자라면 OpenAI SDK 문서에서 sandbox 설정 방법부터 확인해보자. 작은 파일 처리 작업부터 시작하는 게 좋다.

REGISTER NOW . Agentic AI is the tech industry’s newest success story, and companies like OpenAI and Anthropic are racing to give enterprises the tools they need to create these automated little helpers. To that end, OpenAI has now updated its agents software development toolkit (SDK) , introducing a number of new features designed to help businesses create their own agents that run on the backs of OpenAI’s models. The SDK’s new capabilities include a sandboxing ability, which allows the agents to operate in controlled computer environments. This is important because running agents in a totally unsupervised fashion can be risky due to their occasionally unpredictable nature. With the sandbox integration, agents can work in a siloed capacity within a particular workspace, accessing files and code only for particular operations, while otherwise protecting the system’s overall integrity. Relatedly, the new version of the SDK also provides developers with an in-distribution harness for frontier models that will allow those agents to work with files and approved tools within a workspace, the company said. (In agent development, the “harness” is a term that refers to the other components of an agent besides the model that it’s running on. REGISTER NOW . Historically, marketers relied on designers and other creative professionals to develop images and videos for personalized online ad campaigns. In late 2024, seven-year-old startup Hightouch launched an AI-powered service that allows marketing professionals to create custom content for brands such as Domino’s, Chime, PetSmart, and Spotify without involving brand design teams or ad agencies. The offering has been highly successful. Since introducing its AI product 20 months ago, Hightouch has added $70 million in annualized recurring revenue (ARR), it tells TechCrunch, bringing the startup to a total of $100 million in ARR. “Before GenAI, it was impossible for someone without many, many years of design skills to create consumer-level assets,” said Kashish Gupta, Hightouch’s co-CEO. The company is also led by co-CEO Tejas Manohar, a former engineering manager at Segment, a customer data platform acquired by Twilio for $3.2 billion in 2020. However, Hightouch’s approach goes beyond what standard AI models can do on their own. Hightouch says that many brands initially attempted to generate ad campaigns using general foundational models — broad AI systems that power tools like chatbots but lack knowledge of specific brands — only to find the resulting images and videos failed to meet “on-brand” standards. “Foundation models didn’t know about specific consumer brands, whether it was colors or fonts, tone, or a

candidate_articles.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet
candidate_articles.summary: LinkedIn says hiring is down 20% since 2022, but blames higher interest rates — not AI — for the slowdown.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . LinkedIn’s Blake Lawit, the chief global affairs and legal officer of the Microsoft-owned professional networking site, confirmed in an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week that the company’s data shows a decline in hiring of around 20% since 2022. However, he pushed back at the idea that AI was to blame. “At LinkedIn… we have an economic graph which is over a billion members. We’ve got companies, jobs, skills. It’s really an amazing real-time view of what’s happening in the labor market. And we’ve looked — because everyone wants to know the answer to this question: Is AI impacting jobs right now? We’ve looked and, honestly, we haven’t seen it,” he said during his interview. Instead, the executive suggested that the decline in hiring was more closely tied to a rise in interest rates. “We have not seen the sort of impacts that you would expect to see in areas that everyone is talking about AI… like industries, whether or not it’s customer support, or administrative, or marketing — all these places that if we were seeing impacts [from] AI that’s where it would be,” Lawit continued. “Yes, hiring’s down, but not down more,” he added. Lawit also noted that LinkedIn’s data didn’t indicate that the decline in hiring of college-aged young adults getting their first jobs was “down more,” either, when compared with people who were in the middle of or later in their careers. candidate_articles.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
candidate_articles.summary: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong, it's diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is a part of it.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . The role of observability tools has evolved once again. While the market for solutions to ensure tech systems’ reliability has grown over the years, the center of gravity has steadily shifted from “track everything” to “control complexity and costs.” Meanwhile, the rapid influx and adoption of AI agents within enterprises have only added a brand new category of workload that needs to be observed. InsightFinder AI , a startup based on 15 years of academic research, is no stranger to this problem. The company has been using machine learning to monitor, identify, and proactively fix IT infrastructure issues since 2016 , and is now attacking today’s AI model reliability issue with an AI agent solution that can do everything from detection and diagnosis to remediation and prevention. The company, founded by CEO Helen Gu, a computer science professor at North Carolina State University who previously worked at IBM and Google, recently raised $15 million in a Series B round led by Yu Galaxy, TechCrunch has exclusively learned. According to Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong; it’s diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is a part of it. “In order to diagnose these AI model problems, you need to actually monitor and analyze the data, the model, and the infrastructure together,” Gu told TechCrunch. “It’s not always a mod

content.post_main: 앤트로픽이 클로드 코드에 '루틴' 기능을 새로 추가했다. 클로드 코드는 앤트로픽의 개발자용 AI 도구인데, 이제 반복 작업을 자동화할 수 있게 됐다. 한번 설정하면 프롬프트, 코드 저장소, 외부 연동을 기반으로 시간대별/일간/주간 또는 API 트리거로 자동 실행된다. 크론잡 같은 스케줄링을 AI가 대신 처리하는 개념이다.
replies: 클로드 코드 자체는 claude.ai에서 접근할 수 있는 코딩 전용 인터페이스다. 기존 클로드 채팅과 달리 코드 작성/실행에 최적화되어 있다.
루틴으로 할 수 있는 일: 매일 깃허브 이슈 정리, 주간 리포트 생성, 특정 이벤트 발생시 슬랙 알림, 데이터 백업 자동화 등이 가능하다고 한다.
다만 아직 연구 프리뷰 단계라 기능 제한이 있을 수 있다. 크론잡 대비 장점은 자연어로 설정 가능하다는 점, 단점은 외부 의존성이다.
반복 업무가 많은 개발자라면 claude.ai에서 클로드 코드 → 루틴 메뉴 확인해보자. 간단한 자동화부터 시작하면 좋을 것 같다.

candidate_articles.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
candidate_articles.summary: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong, it's diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is a part of it.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits San Francisco. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . The role of observability tools has evolved once again. While the market for solutions to ensure tech systems’ reliability has grown over the years, the center of gravity has steadily shifted from “track everything” to “control complexity and costs.” Meanwhile, the rapid influx and adoption of AI agents within enterprises have only added a brand new category of workload that needs to be observed. InsightFinder AI , a startup based on 15 years of academic research, is no stranger to this problem. The company has been using machine learning to monitor, identify, and proactively fix IT infrastructure issues since 2016 , and is now attacking today’s AI model reliability issue with an AI agent solution that can do everything from detection and diagnosis to remediation and prevention. The company, founded by CEO Helen Gu, a computer science professor at North Carolina State University who previously worked at IBM and Google, recently raised $15 million in a Series B round led by Yu Galaxy, TechCrunch has exclusively learned. According to Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong; it’s diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is a part of it. “In order to diagnose these AI model problems, you need to actually monitor and analyze the data, the model, and the infrastructure together,” Gu told TechCrunch. “It’s not always a mod

기존 제미나이는 웹페이지를 요약하거나 질문하는 정도였다면,

candidate_articles.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
candidate_articles.summary: The studio's first project will be about Moses and star Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley, to be released this spring on Prime Video.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . AI video generation startup Luma has launched Innovative Dreams, a production company built in partnership with Wonder Project, a streaming service that produces religious films and TV on Amazon Prime. The tie-up’s first show will be called “The Old Stories: Moses,” starring British actor Ben Kingsley and set to launch this spring on Prime Video. “Innovative Dreams is a production services company where seasoned filmmakers from director Jon Erwin’s team and Luma’s creative technologists work with great studios and filmmakers to help them realize ambitious ideas,” Luma said Thursday in a social media post . The company envisages creative teams collaborating in real time with Luma Agents to make changes to sets, props, and lighting, as well as bring in footage of human actors. Luma Agents are the company’s recently launched tools designed to handle end-to-end creative work across text, image, video, and audio. “This is a significant improvement over the current virtual production and performance capture processes where things come together only in post,” Luma’s post said. “This is the leverage of AI — not just faster or cheaper, but better than what came before.” Luma isn’t the only startup to move from tooling to production. AI startup Higgsfield last week launched an original series , starting with a 10-minute sci-fi episode, and London-based creative studio Wonder Studios is working on a documentary wi

candidate_articles.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
candidate_articles.summary: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to nab its third funding round since launching just seven months ago, according to Bloomberg . This latest round — which aims to raise around $180 million to $200 million — would value the company at about $2 billion. The company announced a $200 million Series A in January and a $100 million seed round in September, when it first launched. Investors in the company include Tiger Global Management, Xora Innovation, and Premji Invest. Notably, Upscale AI has yet to release a product. However, it’s said to focus on building custom chips and on the infrastructure to enable them to communicate effectively. The company is betting on a full-stack solution and open standards as being the future of scalable AI infrastructure. The rumored valuation and raise are part of the startup playbook in this age of AI, where companies grow fast and valuations grow faster, but the hope for the next big thing outpaces it all. content.post_main: 오픈AI가 생명과학 전용 AI 모델 'GPT-로절린드'를 출시했다. DNA 구조 발견에 기여한 과학자 로절린드 프랭클린의 이름을 따왔다. 가설 생성부터 실험 설계, 데이터 분석까지 수행한다. 생화학, 유전체학, 신약 개발, 전임상 연구 등에서 인간 연구자와 협업하는 'AI 연구 파트너'로 설계됐다.
replies: 일반 GPT와 달리 생명과학에 특화된 지식과 추론 능력을 갖췄다. 복잡한 생물학적 데이터를 이해하고 해석할 수 있다.
신약 개발 패러다임이 바뀔 수 있겠다. 연구 가설 검증이나 실험 설계에서 AI가 실질적 도움을 줄 수 있을 것 같다.
바이오테크 스타트업이나 제약회사에서 활용도가 높을 것 같다. 연구 속도를 크게 단축시킬 수 있을 듯.
AI가 이제 특정 전문 분야로 깊이 들어가고 있다. 의료, 법무, 금융에 이어 생명과학까지 전용 모델이 나오고 있거든.

candidate_articles.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
candidate_articles.summary: The studio's first project will be about Moses and star Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley, to be released this spring on Prime Video.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . AI video generation startup Luma has launched Innovative Dreams, a production company built in partnership with Wonder Project, a streaming service that produces religious films and TV on Amazon Prime. The tie-up’s first show will be called “The Old Stories: Moses,” starring British actor Ben Kingsley and set to launch this spring on Prime Video. “Innovative Dreams is a production services company where seasoned filmmakers from director Jon Erwin’s team and Luma’s creative technologists work with great studios and filmmakers to help them realize ambitious ideas,” Luma said Thursday in a social media post . The company envisages creative teams collaborating in real time with Luma Agents to make changes to sets, props, and lighting, as well as bring in footage of human actors. Luma Agents are the company’s recently launched tools designed to handle end-to-end creative work across text, image, video, and audio. “This is a significant improvement over the current virtual production and performance capture processes where things come together only in post,” Luma’s post said. “This is the leverage of AI — not just faster or cheaper, but better than what came before.” Luma isn’t the only startup to move from tooling to production. AI startup Higgsfield last week launched an original series , starting with a 10-minute sci-fi episode, and London-based creative studio Wonder Studios is working on a documentary wi

candidate_articles.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
candidate_articles.summary: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to nab its third funding round since launching just seven months ago, according to Bloomberg . This latest round — which aims to raise around $180 million to $200 million — would value the company at about $2 billion. The company announced a $200 million Series A in January and a $100 million seed round in September, when it first launched. Investors in the company include Tiger Global Management, Xora Innovation, and Premji Invest. Notably, Upscale AI has yet to release a product. However, it’s said to focus on building custom chips and on the infrastructure to enable them to communicate effectively. The company is betting on a full-stack solution and open standards as being the future of scalable AI infrastructure. The rumored valuation and raise are part of the startup playbook in this age of AI, where companies grow fast and valuations grow faster, but the hope for the next big thing outpaces it all. 기존 제미나이는 웹페이지를 요약하거나 질문하는 정도였다면,

The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It’s dubbed the company a “RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY” full of “Leftwing nut jobs” and a menace to national security. But some of the ice may reportedly be melting between the two, thanks to Anthropic’s buzzy new cybersecurity-focused model : Claude Mythos Preview. Anthropic’s relationship with the Pentagon soured quickly in late February after the company refused to budge on two red lines: using its technology for domestic mass surveillance or lethal fully autonomous weapons with no human in the loop. Anthropic’s tech has in the past been used heavily by the DoD and, it was the first company to have its models cleared to operate on classified military networks. The stalemate led to public insults on social media, Anthropic being categorized as a “ supply chain risk ,” the company filing a lawsuit fighting that designation , and a temporary injunction halting its ban. bottom.post_main: OpenAI가 기업용 에이전트 SDK에 '샌드박스' 기능을 추가했다. 이제 AI 에이전트가 격리된 컴퓨터 환경에서만 작동하고, 특정 파일과 코드에만 접근한다. 전체 시스템은 보호받
bottom.reply_preview: 샌드박스에서 에이전트는 지정된 워크스페이스 내 파일 읽기/쓰기, 승인된 도구 사용만 가능하다. 시스템 파일이나 다른 프로젝트는 접근 불가.

bottom.title: 구글, 크롬에 사용자 반복 프롬프트 저장하는 'AI 스킬' 추가

기존 제미나이는 웹페이지를 요약하거나 질문하는 정도였다면,
bottom.reply_preview: 사용법은 간단하다. 크롬에서 제미나이를 열고 프롬프트를 입력한 다음, '스킬로 저장' 버튼을 누르면 끝.

selected_article.reason: This story about Cursor's massive valuation is still the best choice - it's a major milestone that shows how AI coding is reshaping development. With better explanation of vibe coding and concrete examples, it will be much more actionable for readers.

content.post_main: 커서가 500억달러(약 73조원) 기업가치 평가를 받으며 'AI 코딩 혁명'을 증명했다. 앤드리슨 호로비츠와 스라이브 캐피털이 20억달러 투자를 주도하고, 엔비디아도 전략적 투자를 검토 중이다. 이미 엔비디아 개발자 3만명이 커서를 쓰고 있어서 젠슨 황이 '가장 좋아하는 엔터프라이즈 AI 서비스'라고 할 정도.
replies: 바이브 코딩이란 '로그인 화면 만들어줘'라고 말하면 AI가 즉석에서 HTML, CSS, JS까지 다 짜주는 방식이다. 기획서나 설계 없이도 아이디어만으로 코드가 나온다.
커서 시작하려면 VS Code 확장에서 Cursor 설치하고 Ctrl+K로 AI에게 명령하면 된다. 기존 코드 선택하고 '이거 최적화해줘'만 해도 바로 개선안이 나온다.
실제로 써보면 간단한 기능은 몇 분 만에 완성된다. 기존에 한 시간 걸리던 CRUD API 작성이 10분으로 단축되는 수준이야.
엔비디아 개발자 3만명이 이미 검증한 도구다. OpenAI, Anthropic과 어깨 나란히 하는 AI 스타트업 최상위권까지 올라선 이유가 있다.

media_plan.reason: A demo video showing how to use Cursor's Ctrl+K feature and AI code generation would make the 'vibe coding' concept much clearer for beginners

The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It’s dubbed the company a “RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY” full of “Leftwing nut jobs” and a menace to national security. But some of the ice may reportedly be melting between the two, thanks to Anthropic’s buzzy new cybersecurity-focused model : Claude Mythos Preview. Anthropic’s relationship with the Pentagon soured quickly in late February after the company refused to budge on two red lines: using its technology for domestic mass surveillance or lethal fully autonomous weapons with no human in the loop. Anthropic’s tech has in the past been used heavily by the DoD and, it was the first company to have its models cleared to operate on classified military networks. The stalemate led to public insults on social media, Anthropic being categorized as a “ supply chain risk ,” the company filing a lawsuit fighting that designation , and a temporary injunction halting its ban. 기존 제미나이는 웹페이지를 요약하거나 질문하는 정도였다면,
bottom.reply_preview: 사용법은 간단하다. 크롬에서 제미나이를 열고 프롬프트를 입력한 다음, '스킬로 저장' 버튼을 누르면 끝.

candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening , and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from finance apps to talk shows , a certain shoe company just rebranded as an AI infrastructure play , and Anthropic unveiled a model it says is too powerful to release publicly … but apparently not too powerful to demo to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, and Sean O’Kane dig into what’s actually being built in AI infrastructure, who’s winning the enterprise battle between OpenAI and Anthropic, and more of the week’s headlines. He lives in New York City. You can contact or verify outreach from Anthony by emailing anthony.ha@techcrunch.com . The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It’s dubbed the company a “RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY” full of “Leftwing nut jobs” and a menace to national security. But some of the ice may reportedly be melting between the two, thanks to Anthropic’s buzzy new cybersecurity-focused model : Claude Mythos Preview. Anthropic’s relationship with the Pentagon soured quickly in late February after the company refused to budge on two red lines: using its technology for domestic mass surveillance or lethal fully autonomous weapons with no human in the loop. Anthropic’s tech has in the past been used heavily by the DoD and, it was the first company to have its models cleared to operate on classified military networks. The stalemate led to public insults on social media, Anthropic being categorized as a “ supply chain risk ,” the company filing a lawsuit fighting that designation , and a temporary injunction halting its ban. selected_article.reason: This is more immediately useful for the target audience - a new AI tool that developers and creators can actually use right now. It has clear practical implications and workflow changes that make it highly shareable.

content.post_main: 앤트로픽이 '클로드 디자인' 공개했다. 자연어로 디자인을 설명하면 AI가 초안을 생성하는 방식이다. 최신 모델 '클로드 오퍼스 4.7' 기반으로, 현재 유료 구독자에게 연구 미리보기 형태로 제공 중이다. 어도비나 캔바처럼 복잡한 도구 없이도 프로토타입을 바로 만들 수 있다는 게 핵심이다.
replies: 기존 디자인 도구와 차이점은 학습 곡선이 거의 없다는 것. '빨간색 배경에 로고가 중앙에 있는 명함 디자인해줘' 이런 식으로 말하면 바로 결과물이 나온다고.
개발자들이 프로토타입 만들 때 특히 유용할 듯. 디자이너 없이도 기획 단계에서 빠르게 목업을 뽑아볼 수 있으니까. MVP 개발할 때 시간 절약 효과가 클 것 같고.
어도비나 피그마 독점이던 디자인 도구 시장에 AI가 본격 진입한 신호다. 앞으로는 '도구 사용법'보다 '무엇을 만들지 아는 것'이 더 중요해질 듯하다.

candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening , and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from finance apps to talk shows , a certain shoe company just rebranded as an AI infrastructure play , and Anthropic unveiled a model it says is too powerful to release publicly … but apparently not too powerful to demo to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, and Sean O’Kane dig into what’s actually being built in AI infrastructure, who’s winning the enterprise battle between OpenAI and Anthropic, and more of the week’s headlines. The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It’s dubbed the company a “RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY” full of “Leftwing nut jobs” and a menace to national security. But some of the ice may reportedly be melting between the two, thanks to Anthropic’s buzzy new cybersecurity-focused model : Claude Mythos Preview. Anthropic’s relationship with the Pentagon soured quickly in late February after the company refused to budge on two red lines: using its technology for domestic mass surveillance or lethal fully autonomous weapons with no human in the loop. Anthropic’s tech has in the past been used heavily by the DoD and, it was the first company to have its models cleared to operate on classified military networks. The stalemate led to public insults on social media, Anthropic being categorized as a “ supply chain risk ,” the company filing a lawsuit fighting that designation , and a temporary injunction halting its ban. candidate_articles.summary: 2024년 플로리다의 14세 소년 수엘 세처 3세가 AI 챗봇 '캐릭터닷AI'와 수개월간 대화한 끝에 스스로 목숨을 끊었다.여론은 "위험 콘텐츠를 차단하라"는 쪽으로 수렴됐고 캐릭터닷AI는 미성년자 대상 대화를 사실상 차단했다.하지만 문제는 이것이 전부가 아니다.스탠퍼드 연구팀에 따르면 챗봇 응답의 70% 이상에서 사용자의 비위를 맞추는 '아첨 패턴'이 확인됐다. 특히 사용자의 말을 긍정하며 의미를 확대하는 '반영적 요약'이 36.3%에 달했다.폭력적 사고를 보일 때 챗봇이 부추긴 비율은 33.3%였으며, 자해 언급 시 이를 촉진한

candidate_articles.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
candidate_articles.summary: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . Everyone said AI would kill apps. Instead, new app launches are soaring. According to a new analysis from market intelligence provider Appfigures , worldwide app releases in the first quarter of 2026 were up 60% year-over-year across both Apple’s App Store and Google Play. That percentage was an even higher 80% when looking at the iOS App Store alone. In April 2026 so far, the total number of app releases is up 104% across both stores compared to the same time last year, and up 89% on iOS. As Apple’s Senior Vice President of Worldwide Marketing, Greg “Joz” Joswiak, quipped In a recent interview : rumors of the App Store’s death in the AI age “may have been greatly exaggerated.” These findings come amid concerns that the rise of AI chatbots and agents would ultimately see users turning away from apps — a theory that’s already being floated by those in the industry, like Nothing CEO Carl Pei , who is focused on building a smartphone for the AI era. The New York Times also reported last year on the potential for new computing platforms to eclipse the smartphone, like smart glasses, ambient computing devices, or reimagined smartwatches with AI features. 기존 제미나이는 웹페이지를 요약하거나 질문하는 정도였다면,

content.post_main: 모두가 AI 때문에 앱이 죽을 거라고 했는데, 완전히 반대 상황이 벌어지고 있다. 앱피규어스 데이터에 따르면 올해 1분기 전 세계 앱 출시가 전년 대비 60% 급증했고, iOS 앱스토어만 보면 80%나 늘었다. 4월은 더 극적이다. 전체 스토어 기준 104%, iOS만 89% 폭증했다.
replies: 애플 마케팅 부사장 조즈 조스위악도 '앱스토어 죽음설은 과장됐다'고 못 박았다. AI 챗봇이 앱을 대체하는 게 아니라 앱 개발을 민주화시키고 있다는 분석이다.
핵심은 AI가 누구나 앱을 만들 수 있게 해준다는 점이다. 코딩 경험 없는 크리에이터들이 새로운 앱 골드러시를 이끌고 있다.
흥미로운 건 이런 예측이 이미 나오고 있었다는 것이다. 낫싱 CEO 칼 페이 같은 업계 인사들은 여전히 AI 시대엔 앱보다 다른 컴퓨팅 플랫폼이 뜰 거라고 보고 있다.
개발자나 크리에이터라면 지금이 AI 앱 개발 도구들을 탐색해볼 타이밍이다. 시장이 확장하는 초기 단계에서 기회를 잡을 수 있거든.

candidate_articles.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
candidate_articles.summary: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . Google Cloud on Wednesday announced that its eighth generation of custom-built AI chips, or tensor processing units (TPUs), will be split in two. One chip, named the TPU 8t, will be geared for model training and another, the TPU 8i, is aimed at inference. Inference is the ongoing usage of models, aka what happens after users submit prompts. As you might expect, the company touts some impressive performance specs for these new TPUs compared to the previous generations: up to 3x faster AI model training, 80% better performance per dollar, and the ability to get 1 million+ TPUs to work together in a single cluster. The upshot should be a lot more compute for a lot less energy — and cost to customers — than previous versions. It calls these chips TPUs, not GPUs, because its custom low-power chips were originally named Tensor. But Google’s chips are not a full frontal assault on Nvidia’s future, at least not yet. Like the other giant cloud providers, including Microsoft and Amazon , Google is using these chips to supplement the Nvidia-based systems it offers in its infrastructure. It is not flat-out replacing Nvidia. In fact, Google promises its cloud will have Nvidia’s latest chip, Vera Rubin, available later this year. One day the hyperscalers building their own AI chips (which includes Amazon, Microsoft, and Google) may grow to need Nvidia less, as enterprises move their AI needs to their clouds and port

OpenAI is giving users of its Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans access to cloud-based “workspace” agents available in ChatGPT that can perform business tasks. In its blog post , OpenAI gives examples of agents like one that finds product feedback on the web and sends a report in Slack and a sales agent that can draft follow-up emails in Gmail. These new agents follow increasing interest in agents across the AI landscape, especially after OpenClaw — the AI agent formerly known as Clawdbot and Moltbot that touts itself as the “AI that actually does things” — went viral . OpenClaw founder Peter Steinberger now works for OpenAI . OpenAI is also facing increased competition from Anthropic, which offers its own Claude Cowork agent that can complete tasks for you using files from your computer, as well as a separate platform for making autonomous agents . Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), who led the push to create a new consumer financial regulator in the wake of the 2008 recession, told a crowd at a Vanderbilt Policy Accelerator event in Washington, DC on Wednesday. Warren warned of what she called “striking” parallels to that crisis in the AI industry. While she believes the technology has “enormous potential,” she warned that AI companies’ massive spending and borrowing practices are creating a tinderbox and Congress should step in. Though the AI industry has grown rapidly, Warren said the pace isn’t keeping up with their spending, requiring them to borrow from opaque sources like private credit funds, without the same kind of regulatory oversight that traditional banks face. “If AI companies are unable to increase revenues with lightning speed, they won’t be able to service their massive debt loads,” Warren said. “And because of shady accounting strategies, the first big stumble will have everyone running for the exits,

Humans have been building ping-pong playing robots for decades, such as Omron’s FORPHEUS that challenged amateur competitors at CES 2017. What sets Ace apart from the rest is that the robot, which was developed by Sony’s AI division , is the first that can hold its own against top-ranked human players and occasionally even beat them in matches that follow the official rules of the International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF). AI is already capable of besting humans at games like Chess and Go , but physical games pose a much greater challenge as robots have to be engineered to match the speed and responsiveness of the human mind and body. To be competitive at table tennis, a particularly difficult game with a ball moving at a high speed and spin that can alter its trajectory, Sony’s researchers developed a robotic system with eight joints. content.post_main: OpenAI가 ChatGPT에 '워크스페이스 에이전트' 기능을 추가했다. 비즈니스/기업 요금제 사용자들이 클라우드에서 24시간 작동하는 맞춤형 AI 봇을 만들 수 있다. 웹에서 제품 피드백을 찾아 슬랙으로 보고서 자동 전송하거나, Gmail에서 영업 후속 이메일을 작성하는 식으로 말이다.
replies: 앤트로픽의 클로드 코워크 같은 에이전트 경쟁이 치열해지면서 사용자는 더 다양한 선택권을 갖게 됐다는 게 포인트다.
한 번 만든 에이전트를 팀 내에서 공유할 수 있어서 효율성이 높다. 예를 들어 마케팅팀이 만든 피드백 수집 봇을 영업팀도 쓸 수 있는 식.
다만 클라우드에서 회사 데이터를 처리하는 만큼 보안 정책 검토가 필요하고, 기업 요금제라 비용도 고려해야 한다.
개발팀이라면 코드 리뷰 요약, GitHub 이슈 트래킹, 배포 알림 자동화 같은 반복 업무부터 시작해보는 게 좋을 듯하다.

candidate_articles.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
candidate_articles.summary: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW . Google Cloud on Wednesday announced that its eighth generation of custom-built AI chips, or tensor processing units (TPUs), will be split in two. One chip, named the TPU 8t, will be geared for model training and another, the TPU 8i, is aimed at inference. Inference is the ongoing usage of models, aka what happens after users submit prompts. As you might expect, the company touts some impressive performance specs for these new TPUs compared to the previous generations: up to 3x faster AI model training, 80% better performance per dollar, and the ability to get 1 million+ TPUs to work together in a single cluster. The upshot should be a lot more compute for a lot less energy — and cost to customers — than previous versions. It calls these chips TPUs, not GPUs, because its custom low-power chips were originally named Tensor. But Google’s chips are not a full frontal assault on Nvidia’s future, at least not yet. Like the other giant cloud providers, including Microsoft and Amazon , Google is using these chips to supplement the Nvidia-based systems it offers in its infrastructure. It is not flat-out replacing Nvidia. In fact, Google promises its cloud will have Nvidia’s latest chip, Vera Rubin, available later this year. One day the hyperscalers building their own AI chips (which includes Amazon, Microsoft, and Google) may grow to need Nvidia less, as enterprises move their AI needs to their clouds and port

OpenAI is giving users of its Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans access to cloud-based “workspace” agents available in ChatGPT that can perform business tasks. In its blog post , OpenAI gives examples of agents like one that finds product feedback on the web and sends a report in Slack and a sales agent that can draft follow-up emails in Gmail. These new agents follow increasing interest in agents across the AI landscape, especially after OpenClaw — the AI agent formerly known as Clawdbot and Moltbot that touts itself as the “AI that actually does things” — went viral . OpenClaw founder Peter Steinberger now works for OpenAI . OpenAI is also facing increased competition from Anthropic, which offers its own Claude Cowork agent that can complete tasks for you using files from your computer, as well as a separate platform for making autonomous agents . Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), who led the push to create a new consumer financial regulator in the wake of the 2008 recession, told a crowd at a Vanderbilt Policy Accelerator event in Washington, DC on Wednesday. Warren warned of what she called “striking” parallels to that crisis in the AI industry. While she believes the technology has “enormous potential,” she warned that AI companies’ massive spending and borrowing practices are creating a tinderbox and Congress should step in. Though the AI industry has grown rapidly, Warren said the pace isn’t keeping up with their spending, requiring them to borrow from opaque sources like private credit funds, without the same kind of regulatory oversight that traditional banks face. “If AI companies are unable to increase revenues with lightning speed, they won’t be able to service their massive debt loads,” Warren said. “And because of shady accounting strategies, the first big stumble will have everyone running for the exits,

Humans have been building ping-pong playing robots for decades, such as Omron’s FORPHEUS that challenged amateur competitors at CES 2017. What sets Ace apart from the rest is that the robot, which was developed by Sony’s AI division , is the first that can hold its own against top-ranked human players and occasionally even beat them in matches that follow the official rules of the International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF). AI is already capable of besting humans at games like Chess and Go , but physical games pose a much greater challenge as robots have to be engineered to match the speed and responsiveness of the human mind and body. To be competitive at table tennis, a particularly difficult game with a ball moving at a high speed and spin that can alter its trajectory, Sony’s researchers developed a robotic system with eight joints. candidate_articles.summary: On Friday, Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released a preview of V4, its long-awaited new flagship model. Notably, the model can process much longer prompts than its last generation, thanks to a new design that helps it handle large amounts of text more efficiently. Like DeepSeek’s previous models, V4 is open source, meaning it is available…
candidate_articles.details: The long-awaited V4 model is more efficient and a win for Chinese chipmakers. On Friday, Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released a preview of V4, its long-awaited new flagship model. Notably, the model can process much longer prompts than its last generation, thanks to a new design that helps it handle large amounts of text more efficiently. Like DeepSeek’s previous models, V4 is open source, meaning it is available for anyone to download, use, and modify. V4 marks DeepSeek’s most significant release since R1 , the reasoning model it launched in January 2025. R1, which was trained on limited computing resources, stunned the global AI industry with its strong performance and efficiency, turning DeepSeek from a little-known research team into China’s best-known AI company almost overnight. It also helped set off a wave of open-weight model releases from other Chinese AI firms. DeepSeek has kept a relatively low profile since then—but earlier this month, it effectively teased V4’s release when it added “expert” and “flash” modes to the online version of its model, prompting speculation that the updates were tied to a bigger upcoming release. While the company has become a powerful symbol of China’s AI ambitions, its big return to cutting-edge frontier models comes after months of scrutiny—including major personnel departures , delays to previous model launches , and growing scrutiny from both the US and Chinese governments. So, will V4 shake the AI field the way R1 did? Almost certainly not, but here are three big reasons why this release matters. As with R1 before it, DeepSeek clai

candidate_articles.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
candidate_articles.summary: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.
candidate_articles.details: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Google plans to invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic and support the AI firm’s growing computing needs, Bloomberg reports . The Alphabet subsidiary is committing to invest $10 billion now, at a $350 billion valuation for Anthropic, with another $30 billion to follow if Anthropic hits certain performance targets, according to Anthropic. The promise of investment comes after Anthropic released its latest model, Mythos, to a limited group of partners this month. Anthropic says that Mythos is the company’s most powerful model to date and has significant cybersecurity applications. Due to potential misuse, Anthropic has restricted broader access while it works with select organizations to evaluate and address those risks — though the model has already fallen into unsanctioned hands . It’s also likely expensive to run at scale. The AI race is increasingly defined by access to the compute needed to train and deploy these systems. OpenAI has moved aggressively to secure that capacity through a web of multi-hundred-billion-dollar deals across cloud providers, chip suppliers, and energy, including an expanded deal with chipmaker Cerebras this month. Anthropic has been in a scramble of its own. The company has faced widespread complaints about Claude use limits in recent weeks and responded with a bevy of infrastructure deals.
